ddls:
CREATE TABLE "users" (
  "user_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each user */,
  "username" TEXT /* Username of the user */,
  "email" TEXT /* Email address of the user */,
  "password" TEXT /* Password of the user (hashed for security) */,
  "created_at" TEXT /* Date the user account was created */,
  "updated_at" TEXT /* Date the user account was last updated */,
  "role_id" INTEGER /* ID of the role assigned to the user */,
  "last_login" TEXT /* Timestamp of the user's last login */,
  "failed_login_attempts" INTEGER /* Number of failed login attempts */,
  "account_status" TEXT /* Status of the user account (e.g., active, inactive, suspended) */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("user_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_users_role_id FOREIGN KEY ("role_id") REFERENCES roles ("role_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "roles" (
  "role_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each role */,
  "role_name" TEXT /* Name of the role (e.g., Admin, User) */,
  "permissions" TEXT /* Permissions associated with the role */,
  "description" TEXT /* Description of the role */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("role_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "teams" (
  "team_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each team */,
  "team_name" TEXT /* Name of the team */,
  "description" TEXT /* Description of the team's purpose */,
  "created_at" TEXT /* Date the team was created */,
  "leader_id" INTEGER /* ID of the team leader */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("team_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "user_teams" (
  "user_team_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for the user-team association */,
  "user_id" INTEGER /* ID of the user in the team */,
  "team_id" INTEGER /* ID of the team to which the user belongs */,
  "join_date" TEXT /* Date the user joined the team */,
  "leave_date" TEXT /* Date the user left the team (if applicable) */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("user_team_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_user_teams_user_id FOREIGN KEY ("user_id") REFERENCES users ("user_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_user_teams_team_id FOREIGN KEY ("team_id") REFERENCES teams ("team_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "team_hierarchy" (
  "hierarchy_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for the hierarchy record */,
  "parent_team_id" INTEGER /* ID of the parent team */,
  "child_team_id" INTEGER /* ID of the child team */,
  "hierarchy_level" INTEGER /* Level of the hierarchy (e.g., 1 for top-level teams) */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("hierarchy_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_team_hierarchy_child_team_id FOREIGN KEY ("child_team_id") REFERENCES teams ("team_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_team_hierarchy_parent_team_id FOREIGN KEY ("parent_team_id") REFERENCES teams ("team_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "user_activity_logs" (
  "log_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each log entry */,
  "user_id" INTEGER /* ID of the user performing the action */,
  "action" TEXT /* Description of the action performed */,
  "timestamp" TEXT /* Timestamp of when the action occurred */,
  "resource_id" INTEGER /* ID of the resource affected by the action (if applicable) */,
  "resource_type" TEXT /* Type of the resource affected by the action (if applicable) */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("log_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_user_activity_logs_user_id FOREIGN KEY ("user_id") REFERENCES users ("user_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "user_languages" (
  "language_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for the language record */,
  "user_id" INTEGER /* ID of the user speaking the language */,
  "language" TEXT /* Language spoken by the user */,
  "proficiency" TEXT /* Proficiency level in the language (e.g., Beginner, Intermediate, Advanced) */,
  "certification" INTEGER /* Indicates if the user has a certification for the language */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("language_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_user_languages_user_id FOREIGN KEY ("user_id") REFERENCES users ("user_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "user_trees" (
  "tree_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for the user tree record */,
  "user_id" INTEGER /* ID of the user in the hierarchy */,
  "user_tree" TEXT /* Tree structure representing user relationships */,
  "tree_level" INTEGER /* Level of the user in the hierarchy */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("tree_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_user_trees_user_id FOREIGN KEY ("user_id") REFERENCES users ("user_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "permissions" (
  "permission_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for the permission */,
  "permission_name" TEXT /* Name of the permission */,
  "description" TEXT /* Description of what the permission allows */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("permission_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "role_permissions" (
  "role_permission_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for the role-permission association */,
  "role_id" INTEGER /* ID of the role that has the permission */,
  "permission_id" INTEGER /* ID of the permission assigned to the role */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("role_permission_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_role_permissions_role_id FOREIGN KEY ("role_id") REFERENCES roles ("role_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_role_permissions_permission_id FOREIGN KEY ("permission_id") REFERENCES permissions ("permission_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "sessions" (
  "session_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for the session record */,
  "user_id" INTEGER /* ID of the user associated with the session */,
  "session_token" TEXT /* Token for session management */,
  "created_at" TEXT /* Date and time when the session was created */,
  "expires_at" TEXT /* Date and time when the session expires */,
  "ip_address" TEXT /* IP address of the user's device */,
  "user_agent" TEXT /* User agent of the user's browser */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("session_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_sessions_user_id FOREIGN KEY ("user_id") REFERENCES users ("user_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "notifications" (
  "notification_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each notification */,
  "user_id" INTEGER /* ID of the user receiving the notification */,
  "message" TEXT /* Message content of the notification */,
  "is_read" INTEGER /* Indicates if the notification has been read */,
  "created_at" TEXT /* Timestamp of when the notification was created */,
  "type" TEXT /* Type of notification (e.g., system, alert, message) */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("notification_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_notifications_user_id FOREIGN KEY ("user_id") REFERENCES users ("user_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "two_factor_auth" (
  "auth_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for the two-factor authentication record */,
  "user_id" INTEGER /* ID of the user using two-factor authentication */,
  "auth_method" TEXT /* Method of two-factor authentication (e.g., SMS, authenticator app) */,
  "secret_key" TEXT /* Secret key for two-factor authentication */,
  "enabled" INTEGER /* Indicates if two-factor authentication is enabled for the user */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("auth_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_two_factor_auth_user_id FOREIGN KEY ("user_id") REFERENCES users ("user_id")
)
